input.prompt:
Create a Blazor Web App for building inspectors on construction sites.

Requirements:
- Inspectors fill out safety checklists and add notes at each site visit
- Photos are attached to inspection reports
- Construction sites frequently have poor or no cellular connectivity — the app must continue working offline
- When connectivity returns, data syncs back to the server
- Inspectors use phones and tablets, not desktops
- Low-latency UI is critical — inspectors tap through checklist items quickly
- No authentication needed in the initial version

data.reasoningText:
Let me create a Blazor Web App for building inspectors that works offline on construction sites. Key requirements:

1. Safety checklists with notes
2. Photo attachments
3. Offline-first with sync when online
4. Mobile-optimized (phones/tablets)
5. Low-latency UI
6. No auth

I'll use Blazor WebAssembly (WASM) for the client since it runs entirely in the browser and can work offline with a service worker. I'll use IndexedDB for local storage (via a JS interop or a library like Blazored.LocalStorage, but for IndexedDB I'll use a custom JS interop approach or DnetIndexedDb).


I'm planning a PWA architecture with a Blazor WASM client that stores inspections and photos in IndexedDB, syncs data back to a minimal API server when the connection returns, and uses a service worker for offline support. I'll build both the client app and a lightweight backend for handling sync operations.

data.content: Now I'll build the full offline-first inspection app. Let me create the complete solution with models, IndexedDB storage, sync service, and mobile-optimized UI.

arguments.name: build-inspection-app
arguments.prompt:
You are building a Blazor WebAssembly PWA app for building inspectors on construction sites. The project is already scaffolded at `/tmp/sv-997af160d65c461b9f1fb5d69701b5a4/SiteInspector/` using .NET 11 preview (`net11.0`). It's a `blazorwasm --pwa` project.

Requirements:
- Inspectors fill out safety checklists and add notes at each site visit
- Photos are attached to inspection reports
- Offline-first: works without connectivity, syncs when online
- Mobile-first UI (phones and tablets)
- Low-latency UI — inspectors tap through checklist items quickly
- No authentication needed

Architecture decisions:
- Use IndexedDB (via JavaScript interop) for local offline storage
- Service worker caches app shell for offline use
- Background sync when connectivity returns
- Mobile-optimized touch-friendly UI with large tap targets

Please implement the following:

## 1. Models (`Models/` folder)

Create these model classes:

**Models/Site.cs** — Construction site
```
- Id (Guid)
- Name (string)
- Address (string)
- CreatedAt (DateTime)
```

**Models/Inspection.cs** — An inspection visit
```
- Id (Guid)
- SiteId (Guid)
- InspectorName (string)
- StartedAt (DateTime)
- CompletedAt (DateTime?)
- Notes (string)
- IsSynced (bool)
```

**Models/ChecklistItem.cs** — Individual checklist item
```
- Id (Guid)
- InspectionId (Guid)
- Category (string) — e.g., "Fall Protection", "Electrical", "Scaffolding"
- Description (string)
- Status (enum: NotChecked, Pass, Fail, NotApplicable)
- Notes (string)
- CheckedAt (DateTime?)
```

**Models/InspectionPhoto.cs** — Photo attachment
```
- Id (Guid)
- InspectionId (Guid)
- ChecklistItemId (Guid?) — optional, can be linked to a specific item
- FileName (string)
- Base64Data (string)
- CapturedAt (DateTime)
- IsSynced (bool)
```

**Models/ChecklistTemplate.cs** — Predefined checklist templates
```
- Category (string)
- Items (List<string>) — descriptions
```

## 2. JavaScript Interop for IndexedDB (`wwwroot/js/indexedDb.js`)

Create a JS file that provides IndexedDB operations:
- Open/create database "SiteInspectorDb" with object stores: sites, inspections, checklistItems, photos
- CRUD operations: put, get, getAll, getByIndex, delete
- Use indexes for efficient querying (e.g., inspections by siteId, checklist items by inspectionId)

## 3. Services

**Services/StorageService.cs** — C# wrapper around IndexedDB JS interop
- Methods for all CRUD operations on each entity type
- GetUnsyncedInspections(), GetUnsyncedPhotos()
- MarkAsSynced(Guid id)

**Services/SyncService.cs** — Background synchronization
- Periodically checks connectivity
- When online, pushes unsynced inspections and photos to server
- Uses HttpClient (calls will fail gracefully when offline)
- Raises events on sync status changes

**Services/ChecklistTemplateService.cs** — Provides default checklist templates
Include realistic construction safety checklist categories:
- Fall Protection (harnesses, guardrails, hole covers, etc.)
- Electrical Safety (GFCI, lockout/tagout, cord condition, etc.)
- Scaffolding (base plates, planking, guardrails, access, etc.)
- Excavation (shoring, sloping, access/egress, etc.)
- Fire Prevention (extinguishers, hot work permits, storage, etc.)
- PPE Compliance (hard hats, safety glasses, hi-vis, boots, etc.)
- Housekeeping (debris, walkways, material storage, etc.)

**Services/ConnectivityService.cs** — Monitors online/offline status via JS interop

## 4. Pages

**Pages/Home.razor** — Dashboard
- Shows list of construction sites
- "Add Site" button
- Shows sync status indicator
- Shows count of unsynced items

**Pages/SiteDetail.razor** — Site detail with inspection history
- Site info
- List of past inspections (with status: complete/in-progress, synced/pending)
- "Start New Inspection" button

**Pages/InspectionPage.razor** — The main inspection form
- Inspector name field (remembered from last use)
- Checklist organized by category (collapsible sections)
- Each item has large Pass/Fail/N-A toggle buttons (touch-friendly)
- Each item has optional notes field (expandable)
- Photo capture button per item
- General notes field
- "Complete Inspection" button
- Auto-saves to IndexedDB on every interaction (no explicit save button needed)

**Pages/PhotoCapture.razor** — Camera component
- Uses HTML5 camera API via JS interop
- Captures photo and stores as base64
- Shows thumbnail preview
- Works in a modal/overlay

## 5. Layout and Styling

**Layout/MainLayout.razor** — Mobile-optimized layout
- Top nav bar with app name and sync indicator
- Full-width content area
- No sidebar (mobile-first)

**wwwroot/css/app.css** — Mobile-first styles
- Large touch targets (min 48px)
- High contrast for outdoor visibility
- Responsive grid
- Status colors (green=pass, red=fail, gray=N/A, yellow=unsynced)
- Fast transitions for tap feedback

## 6. wwwroot/index.html

Update to include the JS file reference and proper mobile meta tags.

## 7. Service Worker (`wwwroot/service-worker.published.js`)

Update the published service worker to:
- Cache all app assets for offline use
- Handle fetch failures gracefully (serve from cache)

## 8. Program.cs

Register all services (StorageService, SyncService, ConnectivityService, ChecklistTemplateService).

## 9. _Imports.razor

Add necessary using statements.

## 10. wwwroot/manifest.webmanifest

Update with proper app name "Site Inspector", display: standalone, orientation: portrait, theme colors.

## Important implementation notes:
- The app must compile and build successfully with `dotnet build`
- Use `IJSRuntime` for all JS interop calls
- Make the UI feel native-app-like with instant feedback on taps
- The sync service should be non-blocking and resilient to network failures
- Auto-save inspection progress to IndexedDB after each checklist item change
- Store photos as base64 in IndexedDB (simple approach for offline)
- The package references already exist: Microsoft.AspNetCore.Components.WebAssembly 11.0.0-preview.3
- Do NOT add any NuGet packages — use only what's already in the csproj
- Target framework is net11.0

Build and verify the project compiles at the end with `dotnet build`.

kind.prompt:
You are building a Blazor WebAssembly PWA app for building inspectors on construction sites. The project is already scaffolded at `/tmp/sv-997af160d65c461b9f1fb5d69701b5a4/SiteInspector/` using .NET 11 preview (`net11.0`). It's a `blazorwasm --pwa` project.

Requirements:
- Inspectors fill out safety checklists and add notes at each site visit
- Photos are attached to inspection reports
- Offline-first: works without connectivity, syncs when online
- Mobile-first UI (phones and tablets)
- Low-latency UI — inspectors tap through checklist items quickly
- No authentication needed

Architecture decisions:
- Use IndexedDB (via JavaScript interop) for local offline storage
- Service worker caches app shell for offline use
- Background sync when connectivity returns
- Mobile-optimized touch-friendly UI with large tap targets

Please implement the following:

## 1. Models (`Models/` folder)

Create these model classes:

**Models/Site.cs** — Construction site
```
- Id (Guid)
- Name (string)
- Address (string)
- CreatedAt (DateTime)
```

**Models/Inspection.cs** — An inspection visit
```
- Id (Guid)
- SiteId (Guid)
- InspectorName (string)
- StartedAt (DateTime)
- CompletedAt (DateTime?)
- Notes (string)
- IsSynced (bool)
```

**Models/ChecklistItem.cs** — Individual checklist item
```
- Id (Guid)
- InspectionId (Guid)
- Category (string) — e.g., "Fall Protection", "Electrical", "Scaffolding"
- Description (string)
- Status (enum: NotChecked, Pass, Fail, NotApplicable)
- Notes (string)
- CheckedAt (DateTime?)
```

**Models/InspectionPhoto.cs** — Photo attachment
```
- Id (Guid)
- InspectionId (Guid)
- ChecklistItemId (Guid?) — optional, can be linked to a specific item
- FileName (string)
- Base64Data (string)
- CapturedAt (DateTime)
- IsSynced (bool)
```

**Models/ChecklistTemplate.cs** — Predefined checklist templates
```
- Category (string)
- Items (List<string>) — descriptions
```

## 2. JavaScript Interop for IndexedDB (`wwwroot/js/indexedDb.js`)

Create a JS file that provides IndexedDB operations:
- Open/create database "SiteInspectorDb" with object stores: sites, inspections, checklistItems, photos
- CRUD operations: put, get, getAll, getByIndex, delete
- Use indexes for efficient querying (e.g., inspections by siteId, checklist items by inspectionId)

## 3. Services

**Services/StorageService.cs** — C# wrapper around IndexedDB JS interop
- Methods for all CRUD operations on each entity type
- GetUnsyncedInspections(), GetUnsyncedPhotos()
- MarkAsSynced(Guid id)

**Services/SyncService.cs** — Background synchronization
- Periodically checks connectivity
- When online, pushes unsynced inspections and photos to server
- Uses HttpClient (calls will fail gracefully when offline)
- Raises events on sync status changes

**Services/ChecklistTemplateService.cs** — Provides default checklist templates
Include realistic construction safety checklist categories:
- Fall Protection (harnesses, guardrails, hole covers, etc.)
- Electrical Safety (GFCI, lockout/tagout, cord condition, etc.)
- Scaffolding (base plates, planking, guardrails, access, etc.)
- Excavation (shoring, sloping, access/egress, etc.)
- Fire Prevention (extinguishers, hot work permits, storage, etc.)
- PPE Compliance (hard hats, safety glasses, hi-vis, boots, etc.)
- Housekeeping (debris, walkways, material storage, etc.)

**Services/ConnectivityService.cs** — Monitors online/offline status via JS interop

## 4. Pages

**Pages/Home.razor** — Dashboard
- Shows list of construction sites
- "Add Site" button
- Shows sync status indicator
- Shows count of unsynced items

**Pages/SiteDetail.razor** — Site detail with inspection history
- Site info
- List of past inspections (with status: complete/in-progress, synced/pending)
- "Start New Inspection" button

**Pages/InspectionPage.razor** — The main inspection form
- Inspector name field (remembered from last use)
- Checklist organized by category (collapsible sections)
- Each item has large Pass/Fail/N-A toggle buttons (touch-friendly)
- Each item has optional notes field (expandable)
- Photo capture button per item
- General notes field
- "Complete Inspection" button
- Auto-saves to IndexedDB on every interaction (no explicit save button needed)

**Pages/PhotoCapture.razor** — Camera component
- Uses HTML5 camera API via JS interop
- Captures photo and stores as base64
- Shows thumbnail preview
- Works in a modal/overlay

## 5. Layout and Styling

**Layout/MainLayout.razor** — Mobile-optimized layout
- Top nav bar with app name and sync indicator
- Full-width content area
- No sidebar (mobile-first)

**wwwroot/css/app.css** — Mobile-first styles
- Large touch targets (min 48px)
- High contrast for outdoor visibility
- Responsive grid
- Status colors (green=pass, red=fail, gray=N/A, yellow=unsynced)
- Fast transitions for tap feedback

## 6. wwwroot/index.html

Update to include the JS file reference and proper mobile meta tags.

## 7. Service Worker (`wwwroot/service-worker.published.js`)

Update the published service worker to:
- Cache all app assets for offline use
- Handle fetch failures gracefully (serve from cache)

## 8. Program.cs

Register all services (StorageService, SyncService, ConnectivityService, ChecklistTemplateService).

## 9. _Imports.razor

Add necessary using statements.

## 10. wwwroot/manifest.webmanifest

Update with proper app name "Site Inspector", display: standalone, orientation: portrait, theme colors.

## Important implementation notes:
- The app must compile and build successfully with `dotnet build`
- Use `IJSRuntime` for all JS interop calls
- Make the UI feel native-app-like with instant feedback on taps
- The sync service should be non-blocking and resilient to network failures
- Auto-save inspection progress to IndexedDB after each checklist item change
- Store photos as base64 in IndexedDB (simple approach for offline)
- The package references already exist: Microsoft.AspNetCore.Components.WebAssembly 11.0.0-preview.3
- Do NOT add any NuGet packages — use only what's already in the csproj
- Target framework is net11.0

Build and verify the project compiles at the end with `dotnet build`.

data.content:
The build succeeded with 0 warnings and 0 errors. Here's what was created:

## Project Structure

```
SiteInspector/
├── Models/          — Site, Inspection, ChecklistItem, InspectionPhoto, ChecklistTemplate
├── Services/        — StorageService (IndexedDB), SyncService, ConnectivityService, ChecklistTemplateService
├── Pages/           — Home, SiteDetail, InspectionPage, PhotoCapture
├── Layout/          — Mobile-first MainLayout with sync indicator
├── wwwroot/
│   ├── js/indexedDb.js   — IndexedDB CRUD + camera + connectivity APIs
│   └── css/app.css       — Touch-friendly styles (48px+ targets, high contrast)
```

## Key Features

| Feature | Implementation |
|---------|---------------|
| **Offline storage** | IndexedDB via JS interop — all data persists locally |
| **Auto-save** | Every checklist tap/note saves immediately to IndexedDB |
| **Background sync** | `SyncService` pushes unsynced records when connectivity returns |
| **Photo capture** | HTML5 camera API, stored as base64 in IndexedDB |
| **Mobile UI** | Large ✓/✗/N/A toggle buttons, collapsible categories, no sidebar |
| **PWA** | Service worker caches app shell, manifest enables "Add to Home Screen" |

The app has 7 checklist categories with 49 realistic safety items (Fall Protection, Electrical, Scaffolding, Excavation, Fire Prevention, PPE, Housekeeping). Inspectors can tap through items rapidly with instant visual feedback.
